rawText:
A literature review, contrary to what its name might suggest, is not a passive catalogue of everything that has been written on a topic but an actively constructed argument about how existing knowledge fits together and where it falls short.

The distinction between summary and synthesis — the former merely restating what individual studies found, the latter weaving those findings into a coherent narrative that reveals patterns, contradictions, and gaps — is the single most important concept a novice researcher must grasp.

Summarizing is a necessary first step, yet a literature review that never moves beyond summary resembles a bibliography with commentary: informative, perhaps, but intellectually inert and incapable of advancing the conversation in any meaningful direction.

Synthesis, by contrast, requires the writer to impose an interpretive framework on the accumulated evidence, grouping studies not by chronology or author but by the theoretical questions they collectively illuminate or fail to address.

Accomplished reviewers deploy a repertoire of academic verbs — "corroborates," "complicates," "extends," "challenges," "refines" — each of which positions a new study in precise relation to the ones that preceded it, constructing what scholars call an "evolving dialogue" rather than a static list.

The rhetorical architecture of a strong literature review typically follows a funneling structure, moving from broad consensus to contested terrain and finally to the specific gap that the author's own study proposes to fill.

This gap, far from being an afterthought, is the argumentative engine of the entire review; without it, the reader has no reason to believe that the study being introduced contributes anything that was not already known.

Identifying a genuine gap, however, demands more than locating a topic that has not yet been studied — it requires demonstrating that the absence of knowledge on that topic has consequences for theory, practice, or both, a task that itself constitutes a form of original argumentation.

The tense system in a literature review carries subtle but crucial semantic weight: present tense ("Smith argues") implies that a claim remains current and accepted, while past tense ("Smith argued") may signal that subsequent research has superseded or complicated it.

Hedging expressions — "it appears that," "the evidence tentatively suggests," "there is growing but inconclusive support for" — perform the essential function of calibrating the writer's confidence to the actual strength of the evidence, a skill that separates scholarly prose from journalistic assertion.

Novice writers frequently commit the error of treating all cited sources as equally authoritative, failing to distinguish between a landmark meta-analysis involving thousands of participants and a preliminary case study with a sample of twelve.

Not until a reader can evaluate both the individual merit of each source and the cumulative weight of the evidence does the literature review fulfill its deepest purpose: enabling informed judgment about what we know, what we suspect, and what we have yet to discover.

A well-constructed literature review, therefore, is not a prelude to the real research but an intellectual contribution in its own right, one whose quality often determines whether the study that follows it will be taken seriously by the scholarly community.

Understanding this distinction transforms the act of reading a literature review from a tedious obligation into an exercise in critical thinking that sharpens every subsequent encounter with academic text.

overview.title: What a Literature Review Actually Does

sentences.text: A literature review is not just a list of what other people have written — it is an argument about how all those studies connect to each other.
sentences.translation: 문헌 검토는 다른 사람들이 쓴 것의 목록이 아니에요 — 그 모든 연구들이 서로 어떻게 연결되는지에 대한 논증이에요.

sentences.text: There is a big difference between just repeating what studies said (summary) and showing how they fit together (synthesis).
sentences.translation: 연구들이 말한 것을 그냥 반복하는 것(요약)과 그것들이 어떻게 맞아 들어가는지 보여주는 것(종합) 사이에는 큰 차이가 있어요.

sentences.text: Good academic writers use special verbs like "supports," "challenges," and "extends" to show how each new study relates to older ones.
sentences.translation: 좋은 학술 글쓴이는 "뒷받침한다," "도전한다," "확장한다" 같은 특별한 동사를 써서 각 새 연구가 이전 연구와 어떻게 관련되는지 보여줘요.

sentences.text: A literature review usually points out a "gap" — something that nobody has studied yet — to explain why the new research is needed.
sentences.translation: 문헌 검토는 보통 "공백" — 아직 아무도 연구하지 않은 것 — 을 지적해서 새 연구가 왜 필요한지 설명해요.

sentences.text: Scientists use careful words like "it appears that" because they want to match their confidence level to how strong the evidence actually is.
sentences.translation: 과학자들은 증거가 실제로 얼마나 강한지에 자신감 수준을 맞추고 싶어서 "~인 것으로 보인다" 같은 조심스러운 말을 써요.

sentences.text: A well-written literature review is not boring background work — it is an important piece of research all by itself.
sentences.translation: 잘 쓴 문헌 검토는 지루한 배경 작업이 아니에요 — 그 자체로 중요한 연구 성과예요.

sentences.text: The article argues that a literature review is an actively constructed argument, not a passive list of previous studies.
sentences.translation: 이 글은 문헌 검토가 이전 연구들의 수동적 목록이 아니라 능동적으로 구성된 논증이라고 주장해요.

sentences.text: The key distinction is between summary, which restates findings, and synthesis, which weaves them into a coherent narrative revealing patterns and gaps.
sentences.translation: 핵심 구별은 발견을 재진술하는 요약과, 그것을 패턴과 공백을 드러내는 일관된 서사로 엮는 종합 사이에 있어요.

sentences.text: Academic verbs like "corroborates," "complicates," and "refines" position each study in relation to previous work, creating an evolving dialogue.
sentences.translation: "뒷받침한다," "복잡하게 만든다," "정제한다" 같은 학술 동사는 각 연구를 이전 연구와의 관계 속에 위치시켜 진화하는 대화를 만들어요.

sentences.text: The "gap" in a literature review is not an afterthought but the argumentative engine that justifies the new study's existence.
sentences.translation: 문헌 검토의 "공백"은 부가적 생각이 아니라 새 연구의 존재를 정당화하는 논증적 엔진이에요.

sentences.text: Verb tense in academic writing carries semantic weight: present tense implies a claim is still accepted, while past tense may suggest it has been superseded.
sentences.translation: 학술 글쓰기에서 동사 시제는 의미적 무게를 지녀요: 현재 시제는 주장이 여전히 수용됨을 암시하고, 과거 시제는 대체되었을 수 있음을 시사해요.

sentences.text: A well-constructed literature review is an intellectual contribution in its own right, not merely a prelude to the "real" research.
sentences.translation: 잘 구성된 문헌 검토는 단순히 "진짜" 연구의 서곡이 아니라 그 자체로 지적 기여예요.

sentences.text: This text reframes the literature review from a perfunctory academic ritual into a genre of original argumentation whose rhetorical architecture — funneling from consensus to gap — constitutes an intellectual contribution independent of the study it introduces.
sentences.translation: 이 텍스트는 문헌 검토를 형식적인 학술 의례에서, 합의에서 공백으로 깔때기처럼 좁혀가는 수사적 구조가 그것이 소개하는 연구와 독립적인 지적 기여를 구성하는 독창적 논증의 장르로 재구성해요.

sentences.text: The summary-versus-synthesis distinction functions as the text's central binary, with synthesis elevated to the status of interpretive performance rather than mere information aggregation.
sentences.translation: 요약 대 종합의 구별은 텍스트의 중심 이항 대립으로 기능하며, 종합은 단순 정보 집계가 아닌 해석적 수행의 지위로 격상돼요.

sentences.text: The repertoire of academic verbs — corroborates, complicates, extends, challenges, refines — is presented as a taxonomy of intellectual positioning, each verb encoding a distinct epistemic relationship between studies.
sentences.translation: 학술 동사 레퍼토리 — 뒷받침하다, 복잡하게 하다, 확장하다, 도전하다, 정제하다 — 는 지적 위치 설정의 분류 체계로 제시되며, 각 동사는 연구들 간의 구별되는 인식론적 관계를 내포해요.

sentences.text: The tense-as-epistemology argument — present tense signaling currency, past tense signaling supersession — reveals how grammar itself functions as a hedging device in scholarly prose.
sentences.translation: 시제를 인식론으로 보는 논증 — 현재 시제가 현재성을, 과거 시제가 대체를 신호하는 — 은 문법 자체가 학술 산문에서 완화 장치로 어떻게 기능하는지를 드러내요.

sentences.text: The "Not until" inversion near the conclusion models syntactic complexity while making a claim about evaluative competence, enacting the very mastery it describes.
sentences.translation: 결론 근처의 "Not until" 도치 구문은 평가적 역량에 대한 주장을 하면서 구문적 복잡성을 보여주어, 자신이 묘사하는 바로 그 숙달을 실행해요.

sentences.text: By concluding that understanding the distinction "transforms" reading from obligation to exercise, the text performs its own thesis: knowledge of genre conventions converts passive consumption into active critical engagement.
sentences.translation: 구별에 대한 이해가 읽기를 의무에서 훈련으로 "변환한다"는 결론으로, 텍스트는 자체 논제를 수행해요: 장르 관습에 대한 지식이 수동적 소비를 능동적 비판적 참여로 전환한다는 것이에요.

sentences.original: A literature review, contrary to what its name might suggest, is not a passive catalogue of everything that has been written on a topic but an actively constructed argument about how existing knowledge fits together and where it falls short.
sentences.translation: 문헌 검토는, 그 명칭이 시사할 수 있는 것과는 반대로, 어떤 주제에 대해 쓰인 모든 것의 수동적 목록이 아니라, 기존 지식이 어떻게 맞아 들어가며 어디에서 부족한지에 대한 능동적으로 구성된 논증이다.

grammarElements.text: contrary to what its name might suggest
grammarElements.label: 삽입 양보구 (what 명사절 포함)

grammarElements.text: not a passive catalogue of everything that has been written on a topic
grammarElements.label: 보어 부정부 (관계절 + 현재완료 수동)

grammarElements.text: but an actively constructed argument about how existing knowledge fits together and where it falls short
grammarElements.label: 보어 긍정부 (about + 이중 의문사절)

vocabulary.example: The review should be more than a catalogue of previous studies.
An actively constructed argument requires deliberate choices about structure and emphasis.
The existing literature falls short in addressing this specific question.

expressions.phrase: contrary to what its name might suggest
expressions.meaning: 그 명칭이 시사할 수 있는 것과는 반대로
expressions.usage: 삽입구로 독자의 선입견을 명시적으로 반박하는 학술적 양보 표현. might가 완화를 더한다.
보어 위치에서 흔한 오해를 교정하고 진정한 정의를 제시하는 대비 표현

patterns.pattern: [Subject], contrary to what [source] might suggest, is not [misconception] but [correct understanding] about [deeper question]
patterns.explanation: 삽입 양보구로 흔한 오해의 출처를 지적하고, "not A but B"로 교정한 뒤, about 이하에서 진정한 본질을 밝히는 재정의 패턴.
examples: A thesis defense, contrary to what the word "defense" might suggest, is not a combative interrogation but a collaborative examination about how the research contributes to its field.
Peer review, contrary to what outsiders might assume, is not an adversarial gatekeeping process but a constructive dialogue about how a manuscript can be strengthened.

drillQuestions.question: "Contrary to what its name might suggest" introduces a _____ between expectation and reality.
A literature review is described as an "actively _____" argument.
"Falls short" means the knowledge is _____ in some way.
"Passive catalogue" is contrasted with "actively constructed _____."

sentences.notes: 문헌 검토의 진정한 본질을 재정의하는 도입부. "contrary to what its name might suggest"가 독자의 선입견을 정면으로 반박하고, "not A but B"로 수동적 목록과 능동적 논증을 대비한다.

koreanAlignment.en: A literature review
contrary to what its name might suggest
koreanAlignment.ko: 그 명칭이 시사할 수 있는 것과는 반대로

koreanAlignment.en: is not a passive catalogue of everything that has been written on a topic
koreanAlignment.ko: 어떤 주제에 대해 쓰인 모든 것의 수동적 목록이 아니라

koreanAlignment.en: but an actively constructed argument
about how existing knowledge fits together and where it falls short
koreanAlignment.ko: 기존 지식이 어떻게 맞아 들어가며 어디에서 부족한지에 대한

pronunciationNotes: catalogue: /ˈkætəlɔːɡ/ — 첫 음절에 강세. "캐틀로그"에 가깝다. 미국식 catalog과 동일 발음.
contrary: /ˈkɑːntreri/ — 첫 음절에 강세. "칸트레리"에 가깝다.

sentences.original: The distinction between summary and synthesis — the former merely restating what individual studies found, the latter weaving those findings into a coherent narrative that reveals patterns, contradictions, and gaps — is the single most important concept a novice researcher must grasp.
sentences.translation: 요약과 종합 사이의 구별 — 전자는 개별 연구들이 발견한 것을 단순히 재진술하는 것이고, 후자는 그 발견들을 패턴, 모순, 공백을 드러내는 일관된 서사로 엮는 것 — 은 초보 연구자가 반드시 파악해야 하는 가장 중요한 개념이다.

grammarElements.text: The distinction between summary and synthesis
grammarElements.label: 주어

grammarElements.text: — the former merely restating what individual studies found, the latter weaving those findings into a coherent narrative that reveals patterns, contradictions, and gaps —
grammarElements.label: 대시 삽입 동격구 (독립 분사구문 병렬)

vocabulary.example: Synthesis requires connecting ideas across multiple studies.
A coherent narrative helps the reader follow the argument.
The literature review highlighted contradictions between studies.

expressions.usage: 앞서 언급한 두 항목을 각각 지칭하는 격식적 대비 표현. 학술 영어의 필수 구문.
single이 most important를 극도로 강조하여 유일무이한 중요성을 부각

patterns.pattern: The distinction between [A] and [B] — the former [description], the latter [description] — is [superlative evaluation] [agent] must grasp
patterns.explanation: 두 개념을 대비하고, 대시 삽입구의 "the former / the latter"로 각각을 정의한 뒤, 최상급 평가로 중요성을 강조하는 학술적 정의 패턴.
examples: The distinction between correlation and causation — the former describing a statistical pattern, the latter establishing a directional mechanism — is the most fundamental concept a data analyst must master.
The distinction between compliance and commitment — the former involving external obedience, the latter requiring internal alignment — is the single most critical factor a leader must understand.

drillQuestions.question: "The former" in this sentence refers to _____.
Synthesis involves weaving findings into a _____ narrative.
"The single most important" uses "single" to emphasize _____.
A novice researcher "must grasp" means they are _____ to understand this.

sentences.notes: 글의 핵심 이항 대립(summary vs synthesis)을 정의하는 문장. 대시 삽입구 안의 "the former / the latter" 구문이 독립 분사구문으로 각각을 정의한다. "the single most important"가 이 구별의 절대적 중요성을 강조한다.

koreanAlignment.en: The distinction between summary and synthesis
the former merely restating what individual studies found
koreanAlignment.ko: 전자는 개별 연구들이 발견한 것을 단순히 재진술하는 것이고

koreanAlignment.en: the latter weaving those findings into a coherent narrative
koreanAlignment.ko: 후자는 그 발견들을 일관된 서사로 엮는 것

koreanAlignment.en: that reveals patterns, contradictions, and gaps
is the single most important concept a novice researcher must grasp
koreanAlignment.ko: 초보 연구자가 반드시 파악해야 하는 가장 중요한 개념이다

pronunciationNotes: synthesis: /ˈsɪnθəsɪs/ — 첫 음절에 강세. "신써시스"에 가깝다. th를 /θ/로 발음.
coherent: /koʊˈhɪrənt/ — 두 번째 음절에 강세. "코히런트"에 가깝다.

sentences.original: Summarizing is a necessary first step, yet a literature review that never moves beyond summary resembles a bibliography with commentary: informative, perhaps, but intellectually inert and incapable of advancing the conversation in any meaningful direction.
sentences.translation: 요약하는 것은 필요한 첫 단계이지만, 요약을 넘어서지 못하는 문헌 검토는 주석이 달린 참고문헌과 유사하다: 유익할 수는 있지만, 지적으로 비활성이며 어떤 의미 있는 방향으로도 대화를 진전시킬 수 없다.

grammarElements.text: yet a literature review that never moves beyond summary
grammarElements.label: yet 대조절 주어 (관계절 포함)

grammarElements.text: informative, perhaps, but intellectually inert and incapable of advancing the conversation in any meaningful direction
grammarElements.label: 콜론 뒤 동격 형용사 병렬

vocabulary.example: An intellectually inert review fails to advance scholarly debate.
A bibliography lists sources without evaluating them.
A summary alone is incapable of advancing the research conversation.

expressions.usage: 성장이나 발전의 결여를 비판적으로 지적하는 표현
perhaps의 삽입이 양보를 더하고, but이 핵심 비판으로 전환하는 수사적 구조

patterns.pattern: [Action] is a necessary first step, yet [subject that fails to move beyond it] resembles [diminished comparison]: [partial praise], perhaps, but [devastating critique]
patterns.explanation: 행위의 가치를 인정하되, yet로 그것에만 머무는 경우를 비판하고, 콜론 뒤 "perhaps, but"으로 양보-비판 구조를 완성하는 패턴.
examples: Collecting data is a necessary first step, yet a study that never moves beyond collection resembles a warehouse without a manager: organized, perhaps, but analytically sterile and incapable of generating insight.

Listening is a necessary first step, yet a therapist who never moves beyond listening resembles a mirror with no interpretation: reflective, perhaps, but clinically inert and incapable of guiding change.

drillQuestions.question: Summarizing is described as a "necessary first _____."
"Intellectually inert" means the review lacks mental _____.
The word "perhaps" adds a tone of _____ before the criticism.
"Incapable of advancing the conversation" means it cannot _____ the discussion.

sentences.notes: 요약에만 머무는 문헌 검토를 신랄하게 비판하는 문장. "bibliography with commentary"라는 비유가 핵심이며, 콜론 뒤 "informative, perhaps, but intellectually inert"의 양보-비판 구조가 수사적 효과를 극대화한다.

koreanAlignment.en: Summarizing is a necessary first step
yet a literature review that never moves beyond summary
resembles a bibliography with commentary
informative, perhaps, but intellectually inert
and incapable of advancing the conversation in any meaningful direction
koreanAlignment.ko: 어떤 의미 있는 방향으로도 대화를 진전시킬 수 없다

pronunciationNotes: inert: /ɪˈnɜːrt/ — 두 번째 음절에 강세. "이너트"에 가깝다. 화학 용어에서도 사용.
bibliography: /ˌbɪbliˈɑːɡrəfi/ — 세 번째 음절에 강세. "비블리아그러피"에 가깝다.

sentences.original: Synthesis, by contrast, requires the writer to impose an interpretive framework on the accumulated evidence, grouping studies not by chronology or author but by the theoretical questions they collectively illuminate or fail to address.
sentences.translation: 반면 종합은 글쓴이가 축적된 증거에 해석적 틀을 부과할 것을 요구하며, 연구들을 연대순이나 저자별이 아니라 그것들이 집합적으로 조명하거나 다루지 못하는 이론적 질문별로 분류한다.

grammarElements.text: requires the writer to impose an interpretive framework on the accumulated evidence
grammarElements.label: 동사 + 목적어 + to부정사 목적격보어

grammarElements.text: grouping studies not by chronology or author but by the theoretical questions
grammarElements.label: 현재분사구 (not A but B)

grammarElements.text: they collectively illuminate or fail to address
grammarElements.label: 관계절 (that 생략)

vocabulary.example: An interpretive framework gives structure to diverse findings.
The accumulated evidence points in several directions.
These studies collectively illuminate a neglected aspect of the problem.

expressions.usage: impose가 능동적 지적 행위를 강조. 종합이 수동적 과정이 아님을 암시하는 학술적 표현.

expressions.phrase: not by A or B but by C
expressions.usage: 기존의 분류 기준을 부정하고 새로운 기준을 제시하는 대비 표현

patterns.pattern: [Concept], by contrast, requires [agent] to [intellectual action], grouping [items] not by [conventional criteria] but by [deeper criteria] they [collectively reveal or miss]
patterns.explanation: "by contrast"로 앞 개념과 대비하고, requires + to부정사로 능동적 행위를 요구하며, 분사구문의 "not A but B"로 분류 기준의 전환을 보여주는 패턴.
examples: Analysis, by contrast, requires the reader to construct an evaluative framework around the text, organizing observations not by page order or character but by the thematic tensions they collectively expose or obscure.

Critical thinking, by contrast, requires the student to apply a reasoning framework to the argument, assessing claims not by their authority or eloquence but by the logical structures they collectively rely upon or violate.

drillQuestions.question: "Impose an interpretive framework" means to _____ a structure for understanding evidence.
Studies should be grouped by theoretical _____, not by chronology.
"Collectively illuminate" means the studies _____ reveal something.
"Fail to address" means the studies _____ to deal with certain questions.

sentences.notes: 종합의 본질을 구체적으로 정의하는 문장. "impose an interpretive framework"가 능동적 지적 행위로서의 종합을 강조하고, "not by chronology or author but by the theoretical questions"가 분류 기준의 근본적 전환을 보여준다.

koreanAlignment.en: Synthesis, by contrast
requires the writer to impose an interpretive framework
koreanAlignment.ko: 글쓴이가 해석적 틀을 부과할 것을 요구하며

koreanAlignment.en: on the accumulated evidence
grouping studies not by chronology or author
but by the theoretical questions they collectively illuminate or fail to address
koreanAlignment.ko: 그것들이 집합적으로 조명하거나 다루지 못하는 이론적 질문별로 분류한다

pronunciationNotes: interpretive: /ɪnˈtɜːrprɪtɪv/ — 두 번째 음절에 강세. "인터프리티브"에 가깝다.
illuminate: /ɪˈluːmɪneɪt/ — 두 번째 음절에 강세. "일루미네이트"에 가깝다.

sentences.original: Accomplished reviewers deploy a repertoire of academic verbs — "corroborates," "complicates," "extends," "challenges," "refines" — each of which positions a new study in precise relation to the ones that preceded it, constructing what scholars call an "evolving dialogue" rather than a static list.
sentences.translation: 숙련된 검토자들은 학술 동사의 레퍼토리를 배치한다 — "뒷받침한다," "복잡하게 한다," "확장한다," "도전한다," "정제한다" — 이들 각각은 새로운 연구를 선행 연구와의 정확한 관계 속에 위치시키며, 학자들이 정적 목록이 아닌 "진화하는 대화"라고 부르는 것을 구축한다.

grammarElements.text: each of which positions a new study in precise relation to the ones that preceded it
grammarElements.label: each of which 관계절

grammarElements.text: constructing what scholars call an "evolving dialogue" rather than a static list
grammarElements.label: 현재분사구 (what 명사절 + rather than)

vocabulary.example: This finding corroborates earlier claims about the effect.
Academic writers need a wide repertoire of reporting verbs.
The studies that preceded this one laid the groundwork for the field.

expressions.usage: 군사적 비유(deploy)와 예술적 비유(repertoire)를 결합하여 전략적 어휘 사용을 표현

expressions.phrase: an "evolving dialogue" rather than a static list
expressions.usage: 인용부호 안의 전문 용어와 "rather than" 대비로 이상적 문헌 검토의 성격을 규정

patterns.pattern: [Expert agents] deploy a repertoire of [tools] — [examples] — each of which [precise function], constructing [ideal outcome] rather than [inferior outcome]
patterns.explanation: 전문가의 도구 사용을 대시 예시로 구체화하고, "each of which"로 개별 기능을 밝힌 뒤, "rather than"으로 이상과 현실을 대비하는 패턴.
examples: Skilled negotiators deploy a repertoire of framing techniques — anchoring, bracketing, labeling, mirroring — each of which positions the discussion in a particular direction, constructing what diplomats call a "zone of possible agreement" rather than a deadlock.

Master chefs deploy a repertoire of flavor-building methods — layering, deglazing, reducing, emulsifying — each of which adds a distinct dimension to the dish, constructing what critics call a "unified flavor profile" rather than a collection of ingredients.

drillQuestions.question: "Corroborates" means to _____ or confirm a finding.
"Repertoire" refers to a _____ of available skills or techniques.
An "evolving dialogue" is contrasted with a "_____ list."
"Each of which positions" means every verb _____ a study in relation to others.

sentences.notes: 학술 동사의 전략적 사용을 분석하는 핵심 문장. 다섯 가지 동사 예시가 각각 고유한 인식론적 관계를 내포하며, "evolving dialogue rather than a static list"가 이상적 문헌 검토의 비전을 제시한다.

koreanAlignment.en: Accomplished reviewers deploy a repertoire of academic verbs
koreanAlignment.ko: 숙련된 검토자들은 학술 동사의 레퍼토리를 배치한다

koreanAlignment.en: "corroborates," "complicates," "extends," "challenges," "refines"
each of which positions a new study in precise relation to the ones that preceded it
koreanAlignment.ko: 이들 각각은 새로운 연구를 선행 연구와의 정확한 관계 속에 위치시키며

koreanAlignment.en: constructing what scholars call an "evolving dialogue"
rather than a static list

pronunciationNotes: corroborates: /kəˈrɑːbəreɪts/ — 두 번째 음절에 강세. "커라버레이츠"에 가깝다.
repertoire: /ˈrepərtwɑːr/ — 첫 음절에 강세. "레퍼투아"에 가깝다. 프랑스어 차용어.

sentences.original: The rhetorical architecture of a strong literature review typically follows a funneling structure, moving from broad consensus to contested terrain and finally to the specific gap that the author's own study proposes to fill.
sentences.translation: 강력한 문헌 검토의 수사적 구조는 통상적으로 깔때기 구조를 따르며, 넓은 합의에서 논쟁적 영역으로, 그리고 마침내 저자 자신의 연구가 채우려고 제안하는 특정 공백으로 이동한다.

grammarElements.text: The rhetorical architecture of a strong literature review
grammarElements.label: 주어 (of 수식)

grammarElements.text: moving from broad consensus to contested terrain and finally to the specific gap
grammarElements.label: 현재분사구 (from A to B to C)

grammarElements.text: that the author's own study proposes to fill
grammarElements.label: 관계절 (gap 수식)

vocabulary.example: The funneling structure narrows from general to specific.
The review begins with broad consensus before introducing debate.
The review moves into contested terrain where scholars disagree.

expressions.usage: 넓은 것에서 좁은 것으로 점진적으로 초점을 좁히는 글쓰기 구조를 비유적으로 표현
proposes가 연구의 의도를 완화적으로 표현. 겸손한 학술적 자기 소개.

patterns.pattern: The [architecture/structure] of [strong X] typically follows a [shape] structure, moving from [broad] to [intermediate] and finally to [specific target]
patterns.explanation: 구조적 비유(깔때기)로 텍스트의 전개 방향을 시각화하고, "from A to B and finally to C"로 단계적 축소를 보여주는 패턴.
examples: The organizational logic of a strong grant proposal typically follows a cascading structure, moving from societal need to disciplinary relevance and finally to the specific innovation the project promises to deliver.

The narrative arc of a compelling case study typically follows a telescoping structure, moving from industry context to organizational challenge and finally to the specific intervention the researcher chose to examine.

drillQuestions.question: A "funneling structure" moves from _____ to specific.
"Contested terrain" refers to areas where scholars _____.
The gap is what the author's study "proposes to _____."
"Rhetorical architecture" means the _____ structure of a text.

sentences.notes: 문헌 검토의 전체적 구조를 시각적 비유(funneling)로 설명하는 문장. "from broad consensus to contested terrain to the specific gap"의 3단계 축소가 핵심이다.

koreanAlignment.en: The rhetorical architecture of a strong literature review
typically follows a funneling structure
moving from broad consensus to contested terrain
and finally to the specific gap
that the author's own study proposes to fill

pronunciationNotes: consensus: /kənˈsensəs/ — 두 번째 음절에 강세. "컨센서스"에 가깝다. census와 혼동 주의.
contested: /kənˈtestɪd/ — 두 번째 음절에 강세. "컨테스티드"에 가깝다.

sentences.original: This gap, far from being an afterthought, is the argumentative engine of the entire review; without it, the reader has no reason to believe that the study being introduced contributes anything that was not already known.
sentences.translation: 이 공백은, 부수적 생각과는 거리가 먼, 전체 검토의 논증적 엔진이다; 이것 없이는 독자가 소개되고 있는 연구가 이미 알려진 것이 아닌 무언가를 기여한다고 믿을 이유가 없다.

grammarElements.text: the argumentative engine of the entire review
grammarElements.label: 보어 (비유)

grammarElements.text: without it, the reader has no reason to believe that the study being introduced contributes anything that was not already known
grammarElements.label: 세미콜론 뒤 조건절 (이중 that절)

vocabulary.example: The research gap should not be treated as an afterthought.
The gap functions as the argumentative engine of the review.
The study must contribute something beyond what is already known.

expressions.usage: "far from being"은 강한 부정을 표현하는 관용적 삽입구. 예상과의 간극을 강조.
기계적 비유로 공백이 문헌 검토를 구동하는 핵심 동력임을 표현

patterns.pattern: This [element], far from being [low evaluation], is the [vital metaphor] of [whole]; without it, [agent] has no reason to [expected response]
patterns.explanation: "far from being"으로 과소평가를 강력히 반박하고, 비유로 핵심 역할을 부여한 뒤, "without it"으로 그것의 필수성을 논증하는 패턴.
examples: This introduction, far from being mere throat-clearing, is the navigational compass of the entire essay; without it, the reader has no framework for evaluating the arguments that follow.
This methodology section, far from being a bureaucratic formality, is the credibility foundation of the entire study; without it, the reviewer has no basis for trusting the results.

drillQuestions.question: "Far from being an afterthought" means the gap is _____ an afterthought.
"Argumentative engine" is a _____ comparing the gap to a machine part.
"Without it" introduces a _____ consequence.
The gap explains why the study _____ something new.

sentences.notes: 연구 공백(gap)의 핵심적 역할을 비유와 반실사적 논증으로 강조하는 문장. "argumentative engine"이라는 기계적 비유와 "without it" 이하의 반사실적 추론이 공백의 필수성을 이중으로 논증한다.

koreanAlignment.en: This gap, far from being an afterthought
koreanAlignment.ko: 이 공백은, 부수적 생각과는 거리가 먼

koreanAlignment.en: is the argumentative engine of the entire review
without it, the reader has no reason to believe
koreanAlignment.ko: 이것 없이는 독자가 ~라고 믿을 이유가 없다

koreanAlignment.en: that the study being introduced contributes anything
that was not already known

pronunciationNotes: afterthought: /ˈæftərθɔːt/ — 첫 음절에 강세. "애프터소트"에 가깝다. th를 /θ/로 발음.
argumentative: /ˌɑːrɡjuˈmentətɪv/ — 세 번째 음절에 강세. "아규먼터티브"에 가깝다.

sentences.original: Identifying a genuine gap, however, demands more than locating a topic that has not yet been studied — it requires demonstrating that the absence of knowledge on that topic has consequences for theory, practice, or both, a task that itself constitutes a form of original argumentation.
sentences.translation: 그러나 진정한 공백을 식별하는 것은 아직 연구되지 않은 주제를 찾는 것 이상을 요구한다 — 그 주제에 대한 지식의 부재가 이론, 실무, 또는 둘 다에 결과를 초래함을 입증해야 하며, 이 과업 자체가 독창적 논증의 한 형태를 구성한다.

grammarElements.text: demands more than locating a topic that has not yet been studied
grammarElements.label: 동사 + more than 비교구

grammarElements.text: it requires demonstrating that the absence of knowledge on that topic has consequences for theory, practice, or both
grammarElements.label: 대시 뒤 재진술절 (that절 + 3항 병렬)

grammarElements.text: a task that itself constitutes a form of original argumentation
grammarElements.label: 동격구 (관계절 포함)

vocabulary.example: A genuine gap addresses a meaningful knowledge deficit.
The absence of research on this topic has practical consequences.
Identifying a gap itself constitutes a form of original work.

expressions.usage: 단순한 행위를 넘어서는 더 높은 수준의 노력을 요구할 때 쓰는 학술적 표현
itself가 강조어로, 과업 자체가 독립적 가치를 지님을 나타내는 메타 학술적 표현

patterns.pattern: [Gerund phrase], however, demands more than [simple action] — it requires demonstrating that [absence/problem] has consequences for [domains], a task that itself constitutes [form of original work]
patterns.explanation: 동명사 주어로 과업을 제시하고, "demands more than"으로 기대 이상의 노력을 밝힌 뒤, 대시 뒤 재진술과 동격구로 그 과업의 독립적 가치를 논증하는 패턴.
examples: Formulating a research question, however, demands more than choosing an unstudied topic — it requires demonstrating that answering the question would advance understanding in the field, a challenge that itself constitutes a form of theoretical reasoning.

Writing a compelling proposal, however, demands more than describing what you plan to do — it requires demonstrating that the proposed work addresses a consequential problem, an exercise that itself constitutes a form of scholarly persuasion.

drillQuestions.question: "Demands more than locating a topic" means finding a topic is _____ but not enough.
The gap must have _____ for theory or practice.
"Constitutes a form of original argumentation" means gap identification is _____ in its own right.
"However" signals a _____ to the previous point about the gap.

sentences.notes: 공백 식별의 난이도를 높이는 전환 문장. "demands more than"이 단순한 주제 발굴을 넘어선 지적 노력을 요구하고, "a task that itself constitutes a form of original argumentation"이 공백 식별 자체를 독창적 연구 행위로 격상시킨다.

koreanAlignment.en: Identifying a genuine gap, however
demands more than locating a topic that has not yet been studied
koreanAlignment.ko: 아직 연구되지 않은 주제를 찾는 것 이상을 요구한다

koreanAlignment.en: it requires demonstrating that the absence of knowledge on that topic
has consequences for theory, practice, or both
koreanAlignment.ko: 이론, 실무, 또는 둘 다에 결과를 초래함을 입증해야 하며

koreanAlignment.en: a task that itself constitutes a form of original argumentation
koreanAlignment.ko: 이 과업 자체가 독창적 논증의 한 형태를 구성한다

pronunciationNotes: genuine: /ˈdʒenjuɪn/ — 첫 음절에 강세. "제뉴인"에 가깝다.
constitutes: /ˈkɑːnstɪtuːts/ — 첫 음절에 강세. "칸스티투츠"에 가깝다.

sentences.original: The tense system in a literature review carries subtle but crucial semantic weight: present tense ("Smith argues") implies that a claim remains current and accepted, while past tense ("Smith argued") may signal that subsequent research has superseded or complicated it.
sentences.translation: 문헌 검토에서 시제 체계는 미묘하지만 결정적인 의미적 무게를 지닌다: 현재 시제("스미스는 주장한다")는 주장이 현재적이고 수용되고 있음을 암시하는 반면, 과거 시제("스미스는 주장했다")는 후속 연구가 그것을 대체하거나 복잡하게 만들었을 수 있음을 신호할 수 있다.

grammarElements.text: The tense system in a literature review
grammarElements.label: 주어 (전치사구 수식)

grammarElements.text: present tense ("Smith argues") implies that a claim remains current and accepted
grammarElements.label: 콜론 뒤 첫째 설명절 (인용 삽입)

grammarElements.text: while past tense ("Smith argued") may signal that subsequent research has superseded or complicated it
grammarElements.label: while 대조절 (인용 삽입 + 현재완료)

vocabulary.example: Tense choice carries semantic weight in academic writing.
Later findings superseded the earlier model.
Subsequent research challenged the original conclusions.

expressions.phrase: carries subtle but crucial semantic weight
expressions.usage: "subtle but crucial"의 대비가 표면적 단순함 이면의 깊은 중요성을 강조하는 학술적 관찰 표현
may가 가능성을 완화하고, signal이 간접적 의미 전달을 표현하는 완화적 학술 표현

patterns.pattern: The [linguistic system] carries [contradictory adjective pair] weight: [form A] implies [meaning A], while [form B] may signal [meaning B]
patterns.explanation: 언어 체계의 숨겨진 의미적 기능을 콜론 뒤 대비 구조로 드러내는 메타 언어적 분석 패턴.
examples: The pronoun system in a research paper carries subtle but deliberate rhetorical weight: "we" implies collaborative authority and shared discovery, while "the authors" may signal formal distance or institutional caution.

The modal system in academic conclusions carries understated but pivotal epistemic weight: "this suggests" implies a tentative inference, while "this demonstrates" may signal a confidence claim that invites scrutiny.

drillQuestions.question: Present tense ("argues") implies a claim is still _____.
Past tense ("argued") may signal the claim has been _____.
"Semantic weight" refers to the _____ significance of a linguistic choice.
"Subtle but crucial" means the effect is _____ yet very important.

sentences.notes: 시제가 인식론적 태도를 표현하는 학술 영어의 핵심 특징을 분석하는 문장. 현재 시제/과거 시제의 미묘한 의미 차이가 학술 읽기와 쓰기 모두에 결정적이라는 메타 언어적 통찰을 제공한다.

koreanAlignment.en: The tense system in a literature review
carries subtle but crucial semantic weight
present tense ("Smith argues") implies that a claim remains current and accepted
koreanAlignment.ko: 현재 시제("스미스는 주장한다")는 주장이 현재적이고 수용되고 있음을 암시하는 반면

koreanAlignment.en: while past tense ("Smith argued") may signal
koreanAlignment.ko: 과거 시제("스미스는 주장했다")는 ~을 신호할 수 있다

koreanAlignment.en: that subsequent research has superseded or complicated it
koreanAlignment.ko: 후속 연구가 그것을 대체하거나 복잡하게 만들었을 수 있음을

pronunciationNotes: semantic: /sɪˈmæntɪk/ — 두 번째 음절에 강세. "시맨틱"에 가깝다.
superseded: /ˌsuːpərˈsiːdɪd/ — 세 번째 음절에 강세. "수퍼시디드"에 가깝다. supercede는 오자.

sentences.original: Hedging expressions — "it appears that," "the evidence tentatively suggests," "there is growing but inconclusive support for" — perform the essential function of calibrating the writer's confidence to the actual strength of the evidence, a skill that separates scholarly prose from journalistic assertion.
sentences.translation: 완화 표현 — "~인 것으로 보인다," "증거가 잠정적으로 시사한다," "~에 대한 증가하지만 결론적이지 않은 지지가 있다" — 은 글쓴이의 확신을 증거의 실제 강도에 조율하는 본질적 기능을 수행하며, 이것은 학술적 산문을 저널리즘적 단언과 구별하는 기술이다.

grammarElements.text: — "it appears that," "the evidence tentatively suggests," "there is growing but inconclusive support for" —
grammarElements.label: 대시 삽입 예시 나열

grammarElements.text: the essential function of calibrating the writer's confidence to the actual strength of the evidence
grammarElements.label: 목적어 (of 동명사 수식)

grammarElements.text: a skill that separates scholarly prose from journalistic assertion
grammarElements.label: 동격구 (관계절 포함)

vocabulary.example: Academic writers must calibrate their confidence to the evidence.
The evidence tentatively suggests a positive correlation.
Scholarly prose avoids unqualified assertion.

expressions.phrase: calibrating confidence to the strength of the evidence
expressions.usage: 과학적 글쓰기의 핵심 원칙을 정밀한 비유(calibrate)로 표현. 측정 도구의 보정에서 차용.

expressions.phrase: separates scholarly prose from journalistic assertion
expressions.usage: 두 글쓰기 장르의 핵심 차이를 하나의 기준으로 규정하는 비교 표현

patterns.pattern: [Hedging expressions] — [example 1], [example 2], [example 3] — perform the essential function of [calibrating/matching X to Y], a skill that separates [careful practice] from [careless practice]
patterns.explanation: 완화 표현을 예시와 함께 나열하고, 그 본질적 기능을 비유적으로 설명한 뒤, 동격구로 그 기능이 구별 짓는 두 실천을 대비하는 패턴.
examples: Qualifying phrases — "under certain conditions," "with notable exceptions," "to a limited extent" — perform the essential function of bounding the writer's claims to the actual scope of the evidence, a practice that separates responsible scholarship from reckless generalization.

Epistemic markers — "presumably," "arguably," "it remains to be seen whether" — perform the essential function of mapping the writer's certainty onto the reliability of the data, a discipline that separates nuanced analysis from categorical pronouncement.

drillQuestions.question: "Calibrating" means _____ something precisely to a standard.
"Tentatively suggests" is stronger than "proves" — true or false?
Hedging separates scholarly prose from journalistic _____.
"Inconclusive support" means the evidence is _____ but not definitive.

sentences.notes: 완화 표현의 기능을 "calibrating"이라는 정밀한 비유로 정의하는 핵심 문장. 세 가지 완화 표현 예시가 구체성을 더하고, "scholarly prose vs journalistic assertion"의 대비가 학술 글쓰기의 정체성을 규정한다.

koreanAlignment.en: Hedging expressions
"it appears that," "the evidence tentatively suggests," "there is growing but inconclusive support for"
koreanAlignment.ko: "~인 것으로 보인다," "증거가 잠정적으로 시사한다," "증가하지만 결론적이지 않은 지지가 있다"

koreanAlignment.en: perform the essential function of calibrating the writer's confidence to the actual strength of the evidence
koreanAlignment.ko: 글쓴이의 확신을 증거의 실제 강도에 조율하는 본질적 기능을 수행하며

koreanAlignment.en: a skill that separates scholarly prose from journalistic assertion
koreanAlignment.ko: 학술적 산문을 저널리즘적 단언과 구별하는 기술이다

pronunciationNotes: calibrating: /ˈkælɪbreɪtɪŋ/ — 첫 음절에 강세. "캘리브레이팅"에 가깝다.
tentatively: /ˈtentətɪvli/ — 첫 음절에 강세. "텐터티블리"에 가깝다.

sentences.original: Novice writers frequently commit the error of treating all cited sources as equally authoritative, failing to distinguish between a landmark meta-analysis involving thousands of participants and a preliminary case study with a sample of twelve.
sentences.translation: 초보 글쓴이들은 모든 인용 출처를 동등하게 권위 있는 것으로 취급하는 오류를 빈번히 범하며, 수천 명의 참여자를 포함하는 획기적인 메타분석과 12명의 표본을 가진 예비 사례 연구를 구별하지 못한다.

grammarElements.text: the error of treating all cited sources as equally authoritative
grammarElements.label: 목적어 (of + 동명사구)

grammarElements.text: failing to distinguish between a landmark meta-analysis involving thousands of participants and a preliminary case study with a sample of twelve
grammarElements.label: 현재분사구 (결과: between A and B 대비)

vocabulary.example: A meta-analysis combines data from multiple studies to reach broader conclusions.
Not all sources are equally authoritative in academic writing.
Preliminary findings should not be treated as conclusive.

expressions.usage: "commit"이 오류를 의도적 행위처럼 표현하여 비판의 강도를 높이는 학술적 표현

expressions.phrase: failing to distinguish between A and B
expressions.usage: 분사구문으로 오류의 구체적 결과를 제시하는 비판적 표현

patterns.pattern: [Novice agents] frequently commit the error of [flawed practice], failing to distinguish between [high-quality example] and [low-quality example]
patterns.explanation: 초보자의 전형적 오류를 지적하고, 분사구문으로 구별 실패의 구체적 사례를 대비하는 비판적 패턴.
examples: Novice journalists frequently commit the error of treating all quotes as equally newsworthy, failing to distinguish between a verified expert opinion and an anonymous social media post with no supporting evidence.

Novice investors frequently commit the error of treating all growth metrics as equally meaningful, failing to distinguish between sustainable revenue increase and a one-time windfall with no structural basis.

drillQuestions.question: "Commit the error" means to _____ a mistake.
A meta-analysis is _____ authoritative than a single case study.
"Preliminary" means the study is at an _____ stage.
The error is treating all sources as _____ authoritative.

sentences.notes: 출처 평가 능력의 중요성을 대비를 통해 강조하는 문장. "a landmark meta-analysis involving thousands"와 "a preliminary case study with a sample of twelve"의 극적 대비가 동등 취급의 부적절함을 선명하게 보여준다.

koreanAlignment.en: Novice writers frequently commit the error of
treating all cited sources as equally authoritative
koreanAlignment.ko: 모든 인용 출처를 동등하게 권위 있는 것으로 취급하는

koreanAlignment.en: failing to distinguish between a landmark meta-analysis involving thousands of participants
koreanAlignment.ko: 수천 명의 참여자를 포함하는 획기적인 메타분석과

koreanAlignment.en: and a preliminary case study with a sample of twelve
koreanAlignment.ko: 12명의 표본을 가진 예비 사례 연구를 구별하지 못한다

pronunciationNotes: meta-analysis: /ˌmetə əˈnæləsɪs/ — analysis의 두 번째 음절에 강세. "메타 어낼러시스"에 가깝다.
authoritative: /əˈθɔːrɪteɪtɪv/ — 두 번째 음절에 강세. "어소리테이티브"에 가깝다.

sentences.original: Not until a reader can evaluate both the individual merit of each source and the cumulative weight of the evidence does the literature review fulfill its deepest purpose: enabling informed judgment about what we know, what we suspect, and what we have yet to discover.
sentences.translation: 독자가 각 출처의 개별적 가치와 증거의 누적적 무게를 모두 평가할 수 있을 때에야 비로소 문헌 검토는 가장 깊은 목적을 충족시킨다: 우리가 무엇을 알고, 무엇을 의심하며, 무엇을 아직 발견하지 못했는지에 대한 정보에 입각한 판단을 가능하게 하는 것.

grammarElements.text: Not until a reader can evaluate both the individual merit of each source and the cumulative weight of the evidence
grammarElements.label: Not until 종속절 (both A and B)

grammarElements.text: enabling informed judgment about what we know, what we suspect, and what we have yet to discover
grammarElements.label: 콜론 뒤 현재분사구 (3중 what절)

vocabulary.example: The cumulative weight of the evidence supports the theory.
Each source must be evaluated on its individual merit.
Informed judgment requires evaluating all available evidence.

expressions.usage: "Not until"이 문두에 와서 주절의 주어-동사 도치를 유발하는 강조 구문. 학술 영어의 고급 패턴.

expressions.phrase: what we have yet to discover
expressions.usage: "have yet to"는 "아직 ~하지 못했다"를 격식적으로 표현. still haven't보다 학술적.

patterns.pattern: Not until [agent] can [evaluate/achieve] both [aspect A] and [aspect B] does [subject] fulfill its [deepest/true purpose]: [three-part what clause]
patterns.explanation: "Not until" 도치로 전제 조건을 극도로 강조하고, 콜론 뒤 3중 what절로 목적의 포괄성을 보여주는 수사적 절정 패턴.
examples: Not until a student can navigate both the technical vocabulary of a discipline and the rhetorical conventions of its discourse does academic reading fulfill its transformative purpose: enabling genuine dialogue with what scholars claim, what they question, and what they leave unsaid.

Not until a citizen can assess both the statistical basis of a claim and the political context of its presentation does democratic participation fulfill its ideal: enabling wise choice about what is proven, what is disputed, and what remains unknown.

drillQuestions.question: "Not until" at the start of a sentence triggers _____ in the main clause.
"Cumulative weight" refers to the _____ effect of all evidence combined.
The three what-clauses cover: what we know, what we suspect, and what we have yet to _____.
"Have yet to discover" means we _____ discovered it.

sentences.notes: "Not until ... does" 도치 구문이 글의 수사적 절정을 형성하는 문장. 3중 what절("what we know, what we suspect, and what we have yet to discover")이 지식의 세 가지 상태를 우아하게 분류한다.

koreanAlignment.en: Not until a reader can evaluate
koreanAlignment.ko: 독자가 평가할 수 있을 때에야 비로소

koreanAlignment.en: both the individual merit of each source and the cumulative weight of the evidence
koreanAlignment.ko: 각 출처의 개별적 가치와 증거의 누적적 무게를 모두

koreanAlignment.en: does the literature review fulfill its deepest purpose
koreanAlignment.ko: 문헌 검토는 가장 깊은 목적을 충족시킨다

koreanAlignment.en: enabling informed judgment about what we know
what we suspect, and what we have yet to discover
koreanAlignment.ko: 무엇을 의심하며, 무엇을 아직 발견하지 못했는지에 대한 정보에 입각한 판단을 가능하게 하는 것

pronunciationNotes: cumulative: /ˈkjuːmjələtɪv/ — 첫 음절에 강세. "큐뮬러티브"에 가깝다.
merit: /ˈmerɪt/ — 첫 음절에 강세. "메릿"에 가깝다.

sentences.original: A well-constructed literature review, therefore, is not a prelude to the real research but an intellectual contribution in its own right, one whose quality often determines whether the study that follows it will be taken seriously by the scholarly community.
sentences.translation: 따라서 잘 구성된 문헌 검토는 진짜 연구의 서곡이 아니라 그 자체로 지적 기여이며, 그 품질이 종종 뒤따르는 연구가 학술 공동체에 의해 진지하게 받아들여질지를 결정하는 것이다.

grammarElements.text: is not a prelude to the real research but an intellectual contribution in its own right
grammarElements.label: 동사 + not A but B 보어

grammarElements.text: one whose quality often determines whether the study that follows it will be taken seriously by the scholarly community
grammarElements.label: 동격 대명사 one + whose 관계절 (whether절 포함)

vocabulary.example: The literature review is not just a prelude to the main study.
The scholarly community evaluates research on multiple criteria.
The review is a contribution in its own right.
collocations: a field in its own right
an achievement in its own right

expressions.phrase: not a prelude to X but Y in its own right
expressions.meaning: X의 서곡이 아니라 그 자체로 Y
expressions.usage: 문헌 검토의 독립적 가치를 격상시키는 재평가 표현. "in its own right"가 독립적 가치를 강조.
수동태 미래형으로 학술 공동체의 평가를 표현. 연구의 수용 여부가 문헌 검토 품질에 달려있음을 암시.

patterns.pattern: A well-[past participle] [subject], therefore, is not [diminishing label] but [elevated label] in its own right, one whose quality often determines [consequential outcome]
patterns.explanation: "therefore"로 결론을 명시하고, "not A but B in its own right"로 가치를 재평가한 뒤, "one whose quality determines"로 실제적 결과를 연결하는 결론적 패턴.
examples: A well-designed survey instrument, therefore, is not a preliminary formality but a methodological achievement in its own right, one whose precision often determines whether the data collected will support any meaningful inference.

A well-crafted research question, therefore, is not a bureaucratic requirement but an intellectual accomplishment in its own right, one whose clarity often determines whether the subsequent investigation will produce actionable insight.

drillQuestions.question: "Prelude" means something that comes _____ the main event.
"In its own right" means having value _____.
"Therefore" in this sentence signals a _____.
The quality of the review determines if the study will be taken _____.

sentences.notes: 문헌 검토의 독립적 가치를 선언하는 결론적 문장. "not a prelude but an intellectual contribution in its own right"가 핵심 재평가이며, "one whose quality often determines"가 실제적 결과와 연결한다.

koreanAlignment.en: A well-constructed literature review, therefore
is not a prelude to the real research
but an intellectual contribution in its own right
one whose quality often determines
whether the study that follows it will be taken seriously by the scholarly community
koreanAlignment.ko: 뒤따르는 연구가 학술 공동체에 의해 진지하게 받아들여질지를

pronunciationNotes: prelude: /ˈpreljuːd/ — 첫 음절에 강세. "프렐류드"에 가깝다. 음악 용어에서 차용.
scholarly: /ˈskɑːlərli/ — 첫 음절에 강세. "스칼러리"에 가깝다.

sentences.original: Understanding this distinction transforms the act of reading a literature review from a tedious obligation into an exercise in critical thinking that sharpens every subsequent encounter with academic text.
sentences.translation: 이 구별을 이해하는 것은 문헌 검토를 읽는 행위를 지루한 의무에서 학술 텍스트와의 모든 후속 만남을 날카롭게 하는 비판적 사고 훈련으로 변환한다.

grammarElements.text: the act of reading a literature review
grammarElements.label: 목적어

grammarElements.text: into an exercise in critical thinking that sharpens every subsequent encounter with academic text
grammarElements.label: 전치사구 (도착점, 관계절 포함)

vocabulary.example: Understanding genre conventions transforms how we read.
Without proper understanding, reading reviews feels tedious.
This skill sharpens every subsequent encounter with academic text.

expressions.phrase: transforms X from A into B
expressions.usage: "from A into B"가 변화의 출발점과 도착점을 명시하는 변환 표현
every가 보편성을, subsequent가 시간적 연쇄를 강조하여 지속적 영향을 표현

patterns.pattern: Understanding [distinction/concept] transforms [activity] from [negative framing] into [positive framing] that [ongoing benefit]
patterns.explanation: 동명사 주어로 이해의 행위를 주제화하고, "from A into B"로 변환을 보여준 뒤, 관계절로 지속적 이점을 밝히는 변환적 결론 패턴.
examples: Understanding rhetorical structure transforms the act of listening to a speech from passive absorption into active deconstruction that enhances every subsequent encounter with public discourse.

Understanding experimental design transforms the act of reading a study from mechanical comprehension into methodological evaluation that enriches every subsequent engagement with scientific literature.

drillQuestions.question: The distinction transforms reading from a "tedious _____" into an exercise.
"Transforms from A into B" describes a fundamental _____.
"Sharpens every subsequent encounter" means each future reading becomes more _____.
"Understanding this distinction" is the _____ that triggers the transformation.

sentences.notes: 글의 최종 결론으로, 이해의 변환적 힘을 선언한다. "from a tedious obligation into an exercise in critical thinking"의 변환이 글 전체의 목적 — 문헌 검토에 대한 인식 전환 — 을 직접 실현한다.

koreanAlignment.en: Understanding this distinction
transforms the act of reading a literature review
from a tedious obligation
into an exercise in critical thinking
that sharpens every subsequent encounter with academic text
koreanAlignment.ko: 학술 텍스트와의 모든 후속 만남을 날카롭게 하는

pronunciationNotes: tedious: /ˈtiːdiəs/ — 첫 음절에 강세. "티디어스"에 가깝다.
subsequent: /ˈsʌbsɪkwənt/ — 첫 음절에 강세. "섭시퀀트"에 가깝다.
